title: Task: Propose the best repo-local design to make x-auto skills single-SSOT and accessible from both Claude and Codex. Inspect docs/agents/shared-skills.md, current skill sync scripts, and local loaders. Do not edit files. Return a concise recommendation with concrete file changes needed and why.
summary_text: Current state (from inspection): - The shared-skills policy is clear that Codex/Claude capability parity should come from the same manifest, with sync scripts using `--target both` ([`docs/agents/shared-skills.md`](./docs/agents/shared-skills.md), [`scripts/skills/sync-openclaw-skills.sh`](./scripts/skills/sync-openclaw-skills.sh), [`scripts/skills/sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh`](./scripts/skills/sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh)). - `skills/x-auto-post-guard` and `skills/x-auto-thumbnail-art-director` already live in-repo but are not part of those manifests, so they are outside the SSOT manifest flow. - The Codex skill launcher resolves from repo-local `.codex/skills` and `~/.codex/skills` only, not the repo `skills/` root ([`scripts/local/launchers/codex-skill-launch`](./scripts/local/launchers/codex-skill-launch)). - Skill shim/prompt generation currently discovers skills from `~/.codex/skills` + plugin cache, so repo-local skill roots are not surfaced there ([`scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh`](./scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh)). - Evaluation path for x-auto is still Claude-path hardcoded (`~/.claude/skills`), so Codex-only paths are not portable ([`scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml`](./scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml)). Recommendation (best repo-local single-SSOT design): 1. Add a repo-local skill manifest for internal skills: - New file: [`config/skills/fugue-repo-skills-baseline.tsv`](./config/skills/fugue-repo-skills-baseline.tsv) - Include: `x-auto-post-guard`, `x-auto-thumbnail-art-director` with required/optional profiles. - Why: preserves SSOT at repo level and lets policy (required/optional) be centrally governed like third-party skills. 2. Add a repo-skill sync path using the existing dual-target model: - New script: [`scripts/skills/sync-fugue-local-skills.sh`](./scripts/skills/) - Behavior: copy from `skills/<skill_name>` in-repo to `CODEX_SKILLS_DIR` and `CLAUDE_SKILLS_DIR` with managed marker (same safety semantics as existing scripts). - Why: enforces one command to install identical x-auto skill content into both runtimes. 3. Update existing skill profile docs to include the local manifest and script: - [`docs/agents/shared-skills.md`](./docs/agents/shared-skills.md) - [`docs/fugue-skills-profile.md`](./docs/fugue-skills-profile.md) - Why: keeps runtime policy and bootstrap docs consistent with the SSOT rule they already state. 4. Update Codex local loader search order to include repo-local source: - [`scripts/local/launchers/codex-skill-launch`](./scripts/local/launchers/codex-skill-launch) - Add `${repo_root}/skills/<skill_name>` in `find_skill_dir` candidate list. - Why: makes x-auto skills usable immediately from repo context even before any install step. 5. Include repo-local skills in launcher prompt/shim inventory: - [`scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh`](./scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh) - Extend `enumerate_skill_specs` to scan `./skills/*/SKILL.md` (repo root) as a source of discoverable skills. - Why: keeps `/skill` workflow and optional shims/prompt generation aligned with repo-local SSOT skills. 6. Remove Claude-only hardcoding in x-auto evaluation definition: - [`scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml`](./scripts/eval/defs/x-auto.yaml) - Point to a repo-local executable (or environment-driven path), not `~/.claude/skills/...`. - Why: evaluator and CI paths should not depend on only one orchestrator’s home-skill layout. Why this is the safest design - It keeps a single canonical source (`skills/` + new manifest) while still installing to both runtime-specific skill roots required by each orchestrator. - It reduces manual drift (no one-off local copies in `~/.claude` and `~/.codex`). - It preserves the current shared-skills doctrine instead of introducing a separate, disconnected sync pathway for x-auto.
